segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a montage of quick, stylized clips, set to an upbeat, slightly melancholic instrumental track. These visuals include a kitchen counter with jars, a hand plugging in a device, a person in a pink fuzzy jacket at a stadium, and a bedroom with a clothing rack and laptop. Text overlays introduce the theme: 'a few days in the life of a software engineer' with features like 'childhood', 'gym', and 'apt updates'. As the music fades slightly, the scene transitions to a softly lit bathroom on 'friday sept 24, 2021', where a woman in a beige plush robe, with dark hair pulled back and wearing a delicate silver necklace, stands before a mirror. On-screen text requests viewers to 'pls like and subscribe if you enjoy :)'. At '10:00 am', she begins her skincare routine, speaking to the camera in a clear, conversational female voice. She asks viewers to "ignore the fan" and explains she'll walk them through her makeup routine since she didn't film earlier. She uses a cotton pad to wipe her face, then applies a product from a small white bottle, gently massaging it into her cheeks, nose, and forehead. She identifies one product as "Supergoop Unseen Sunscreen," which she uses as her primer every day, even without makeup. Her movements are deliberate and calm as she explains that she applies makeup on most days due to "hereditary dark circles" or a "darker eye area," including her eyelids, which she notes might not be fully apparent in the current lighting. The intimate atmosphere focuses on her morning self-care ritual amidst the visible white shower curtain and floral-patterned towel.

She details her ingredients, mentioning "water, a little bit of milk, and a little bit of sweetener," specifically using "maple syrup" or "vanilla creamer," and about "a teaspoon" of sweetener. She then uses a black electric blender, which she calls her "Magic Bullet," to mix the contents, accompanied by a distinct blending sound, resulting in a vibrant green smoothie. The scene transitions to a close-up of the green smoothie being poured into a 'magic BULLET' glass jar, with the sound of pouring liquid, and an orange straw inserted. The video then cuts to a dimly lit living room where the same woman, now seated, speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. She apologizes if her "matcha" method "insults" viewers, explaining it "still tastes good" and is "easy and fast," before outlining her plans to "drink this, get some more work done," and attend a "meeting for our demos."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a first-person 'apt cam' perspective, displaying on-screen UI elements like a battery indicator. A woman's voice states, "this is what we're looking like now," as the camera pans across a neatly arranged bedroom. She explains, "I decided to put the bed against the window and it's actually been a great decision," referring to the gray headboard bed with white bedding. She points out, "Put two of the pictures that I had on my walls up and then the third one is there," indicating framed artwork, and mentions plans for "a new print" for the bathroom. A hand enters the frame, gesturing toward the bed, then highlights a white fluffy rug on the floor, which the woman describes as "this white faux fur rug. It's really soft. It's actually very supportive too. I thought it was going to be like super thin, but it isn't," adding she thinks she got it "from Overstock." The camera continues to pan, revealing a white clothing rack filled with pastel and neutral-toned garments, a small white shelf, and a framed abstract artwork. The woman declares, "this is the cutest area here. I got a clothing rack," and notes, "I had this mirror already," referring to a mirror reflecting her. She explains she "decided to put it here," and plans to get "a neon sign in the mail" to put "above the clothing rack," believing "it'll look cute." She also intends to get "a picture, something large to hang up here to cover up the electrical stuff." The woman, wearing a gray 'LAKE TAHOE' sweatshirt, black shorts, and a pink smartwatch, holds a black DSLR camera, waving and smiling at her reflection. She expresses, "I am obsessed with this area. It's so cute," and believes "once I hide the electrical unit, it'll be even cuter." The camera moves toward a closet with a mirrored door, revealing a black dresser with gold handles inside. The woman identifies it, saying, "This is my closet, so this is where the dresser went." Her reflection is visible as she continues filming, capturing the apartment’s minimalist and cozy aesthetic. She mentions, "Don't know where I'm hanging up this guy yet," possibly referring to another decorative item. The scene transitions to a round mirror with a gold frame, reflecting the woman crouching and filming, with a glimpse of the kitchen and living area. She concludes, "I'm totally obsessed with my room now and I was scared that I wasn't going to like it in this new place, especially after the initial look of," emphasizing her satisfaction with the bright, clean, and modern space.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a screen recording of the Desenio website, showcasing the 'Ana Johnson Gallery Wall' in the 'Gallery Walls' section. The layout features a curated collection of seven framed art prints in neutral tones, including abstract shapes, typographic quotes, and minimalist line art, displayed above a dark sofa with a woman in a white outfit and hat. The woman viewing the site comments, "I like this piece of a woman, I like this palm one," and continues, "I really like the quote. I'm thinking I will get a portion of this gallery wall." The website interface highlights individual posters with prices and 'Customize' buttons, such as 'JE SUIS COMME JE SUIS' and 'ESTHETIQUE DE L'ART', priced between $50.32 and $86.51. The total for the gallery wall is shown as $534.72, prompting her to exclaim, "It's $534. That's so expensive." The scene then transitions to a woman in a light blue ribbed sweater, lounging on a sofa with a pink velvet pillow and a beige blanket, using a silver laptop. She appears engaged with the Desenio website, navigating through different gallery wall options, including the 'Different Shapes Gallery Wall' priced at $461.92, articulating, "So essentially, I want this layout for above my couch. This one, I think will be really cute." She continues to browse, adding, "I wanted most of the prints from this view," and specifies her preference, "I also wanted black frames, so I don't want the light frames." After 20 minutes, she is shown with a frustrated expression, running her hand through her hair, indicating decision fatigue. The video then cuts back to the website, where she compares the 'Different Shapes Gallery Wall' with the 'Ana Johnson Gallery Wall', side-by-side, highlighting the differences in frame styles, artwork, and overall aesthetic. She reiterates, "It's going to be this layout, remember that. All the frames are going to be black like in this one."
